mission.title: keycloak: Consistent behaviour for User API getUsers and count
mission.description: The `/admin/realms/{realm}/users` and `/admin/realms/{realm}/users/count` endpoints have inconsistent parameter support. The count endpoint was missing filters like enabled, emailVerified, idpAlias, idpUserId, and exact that the users endpoint supports. This issue affects 22+ users.

steps.title: Identify the inconsistent API behavior
steps.description:
Verify the issue by comparing results from the User API endpoints:
```bash
# Check your Keycloak version:
docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config

# Use kcadm.sh to query users vs count with the same filters:
docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh get users \
  -r myrealm --query enabled=false

docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh get users/count \
  -r myrealm --query enabled=false
```
If the count does not reflect the same filtering as the users list, you are affected by this inconsistency.

steps.title: Understand the root cause
steps.description: The `/users` endpoint supported filtering by `emailVerified`, `enabled`, `idpAlias`, `idpUserId`, and `exact`, but the `/users/count` endpoint did not accept or process these parameters. This caused the count to return totals that did not match the filtered user list.

steps.title: Apply the fix for consistent User API behavior
steps.description:
The fix adds support for the missing filter parameters to the `/users/count` endpoint:
1. `/users` endpoint: Added support for filtering by `emailVerified`
2. `/users/count` endpoint: Added support for `enabled`, `emailVerified`, `idpAlias`, `idpUserId`, and `exact` filters to align with the `/users` endpoint

Upgrade to a Keycloak version that includes this fix. See the [source PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/40265) for implementation details.

steps.title: Confirm consistent API behavior is restored
steps.description:
Verify the fix by comparing filtered results:
```bash
# Query users with a filter:
docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh get users \
  -r myrealm --query enabled=false

# Count should now match:
docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh get users/count \
  -r myrealm --query enabled=false
```
Confirm that the count matches the number of users returned with the same filter parameters.

resolution.summary: The `/users/count` endpoint was missing filter parameters that the `/users` endpoint supported. The fix adds `enabled`, `emailVerified`, `idpAlias`, `idpUserId`, and `exact` filter support to the count endpoint, ensuring both endpoints return consistent results when using the same query parameters.

prerequisites.description: A running Keycloak server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes) with Admin API access.
